page: Amazon Web Services c7gd.2xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors. It features 8 dedicated physical cores on the ARM64 architecture, specifically utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V1 model running at 2.6 GHz with 1.0 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Local storage is bundled as a 474 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 4 Gbps. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations place the c7gd.2xlarge in the average performance tier for most compute and memory operations. Single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yield average scores of 1532.19 and 12241.51 respectively. Memory bandwidth is solid, with read-only cached small blocks reaching 775.35 GB/sec and uncached large blocks achieving 148.04 GB/sec. In application-specific tests, the instance demonstrates capable throughput, processing 12.54 million Redis SET operations per second and delivering 15.36 GB/sec in static web serving. However, cryptographic performance is relatively weak, as shown by a PassMark encryption score of 1110.82. Large language model inference is also limited, with prompt processing for a 7B model falling into the poor performance tier at 14.12 tokens per second.

The ARM64-based Graviton architecture offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads that can leverage parallel ARM execution without requiring x86-specific instruction sets. The inclusion of a fast 474 GB local NVMe SSD makes this instance highly suitable for applications requiring low-latency scratch space or high-speed caching. Optimal workloads include distributed data processing, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that benefit from local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.2xlarge is a compute-optimized instance built on the ARM64 architecture and powered by an 8-core AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 16.0 GB of system memory and includes a 474 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate average performance across standard compute and memory tasks, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 12241.51 and a Redis SET throughput of 12.54 million operations per second. While cryptographic and large language model inference speeds are relatively weak, the instance offers excellent qualitative cost efficiency for ARM-native workloads. The combination of dedicated compute cores and high-speed local storage makes it well-suited for software compilation, static web serving, and database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 8 Graviton cores, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering balanced performance for database and web workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gd.2xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 8 Graviton cores, 16.0 GB RAM, and a 474 GB local NVMe SSD.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and high-speed local NVMe SSD storage for efficient, low-latency workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 cores running at 2.6 GHz
16.0 GB of system memory and 474 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Average performance in software compilation, web serving, and Redis operations
Best suited for ARM-native databases, web servers, and development workloads
